Title: Successful User Registration
Preconditions: User is on the registration form page.
Test Data: Valid username, valid email, valid password, valid confirmation password
Test Steps:
1. User enters a valid username in the username field.
2. User enters a valid email address in the email field.
3. User enters a valid password in the password field.
4. User re-enters the same password in the confirmation password field.
5. User clicks the "Register" button.
Expected Result: User account is successfully created, and a confirmation message is displayed.

Title: Successful User Login
Preconditions: User is on the login form page.
Test Steps:
1. User enters a valid username in the username field.
2. User enters the correct password in the password field.
3. User clicks the "Login" button.
Expected Result: User is successfully logged in, and the system redirects to the user’s dashboard or home page.

Title: Failed User Login with Incorrect Password
Preconditions: User is on the login form page.
Test Steps:
1. User enters a valid username in the username field.
2. User enters an incorrect password in the password field.
3. User clicks the "Login" button.
Expected Result: System displays an error message indicating incorrect credentials.

Title: Successful City Search
Preconditions: User is on the city search page.
Test Steps:
1. User enters a valid city name in the search field.
2. User clicks the "Search" button.
Expected Result: System displays a list of POI associated with the searched city.

Title: Display Basic City Information
Preconditions: User has searched for a city and selected it from the results.
Test Steps:
1. User searches for a city (e.g., Venice).
2. User selects the city from the search results.
Expected Result: System displays basic information about the city (e.g., population, location, or other open data).

Title: Successful POI Search by Type
Preconditions: User is on the POI search page.
Test Data: Valid city name, valid POI type (e.g., cycling path)
Test Steps:
1. User selects a city from the dropdown or search field.
2. User selects a POI type (e.g., cycling path).
3. User clicks the "Search" button.
Expected Result: System displays a list of POI of the selected type within the city.

Title: Display POI Details
Preconditions: User has searched for POI and selected one from the results.
Test Data: Venice, cycling path (or any valid POI)
Test Steps:
1. User searches for a city (e.g., Venice).
2. User searches for a specific POI type (e.g., cycling path).
3. User selects a POI from the list.
Expected Result: System displays detailed information about the selected POI (e.g., description, location, coordinates).

Title: Successful Event Insertion
Preconditions: User is logged in and on the event insertion page.
Test Data: Valid event name, valid date, valid description, valid city
Test Steps:
1. User selects a city from the dropdown.
2. User enters an event name in the event name field.
3. User enters a valid date in the date field.
4. User enters a description in the description field.
5. User clicks the "Insert Event" button.
Expected Result: Event is successfully added to the system, and a confirmation message is displayed.

Title: Display List of Events for a City
Preconditions: User is logged in or logged out and on the events page.
Test Steps:
1. User selects a city from the dropdown or search field.
2. User clicks the "View Events" button.
Expected Result: System displays a list of events associated with the selected city.

Title: Successful Event Modification
Preconditions: User is logged in and has inserted an event previously.
Test Data: Existing event, modified event name, modified date, modified description
Test Steps:
1. User selects the event to modify from the list.
2. User modifies the event name, date, or description.
3. User clicks the "Save Changes" button.
Expected Result: Event details are updated successfully, and a confirmation message is displayed.

Title: Successful Event Deletion
Preconditions: User is logged in and has inserted an event previously.
Test Steps:
1. User selects the event to delete from the list.
2. User clicks the "Delete Event" button.
3. User confirms deletion in the confirmation dialog.
Expected Result: Event is removed from the system, and a confirmation message is displayed.

Title: Successful User Registration
Preconditions: Application installed on Android device
Test Data: Valid name, surname, date of birth, email, password
Test Steps:
1. Launch the application for the first time after installation.
2. Select the option to register a new account.
3. Fill in the registration form with valid name, surname, date of birth, email, and password.
4. Confirm the registration by submitting the form.
Expected Result: User data is saved in the database, and the user is prompted to log in.

Title: Registration with Existing Email
Preconditions: Application installed on Android device
Test Data: Email already registered in the system
Test Steps:
1. Launch the application for the first time after installation.
2. Attempt to register with an email already associated with an existing account.
3. Submit the registration form.
Expected Result: System displays an error message indicating the email is already in use.

Title: Successful User Login
Preconditions: Application installed on Android device, user account exists
Test Steps:
1. Launch the application.
2. Select the option to log in.
3. Enter valid email and password.
4. Submit the login form.
Expected Result: User is granted access to the main application functions.

Title: Login with Invalid Credentials
Preconditions: Application installed on Android device
Test Steps:
1. Launch the application.
2. Select the option to log in.
3. Enter an invalid email or incorrect password.
4. Submit the login form.
Expected Result: System displays an error message indicating invalid credentials.

Title: Search for Non-Existent City
Preconditions: User logged in
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the city search feature.
2. Enter an invalid city name in the search field.
3. Press the search button.
Expected Result: System displays a message indicating no results found or prompts the user to refine the search.

Title: Search for Non-Existent POI Type
Preconditions: User logged in
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the POI search feature.
2. Select the option to search by POI type.
3. Attempt to choose an invalid POI type.
4. Press the search button.
Expected Result: System displays a message indicating no results found or prompts the user to select a valid type.

Title: Event Insertion with Invalid Date
Preconditions: User logged in, city searched and selected
Test Data: Event name, type, invalid date (e.g., past date), optional description
Test Steps:
1. Search for and select a city.
2. Press the button to add an event.
3. Fill in the event form with valid event name, type, invalid date, and optional description.
4. Submit the form.
Expected Result: System displays an error message indicating the date must be valid (future date).

Title: View Events in a City
Preconditions: User logged in, city searched and selected, events exist in the city
Test Steps:
1. Search for and select a city with existing events.
2. Navigate to the events section for the city.
Expected Result: System displays a list of all events present in the selected city.

Title: Successful User Login
Preconditions: User must be registered (RF-01) and have valid credentials
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the login screen
2. Enter valid username and password
3. Click the login button
Expected Result: User is redirected to the main application dashboard without errors

Title: Successful User Logout
Preconditions: User must be registered and logged in
Test Steps: 1. Click the logout button
Expected Result: User is redirected to the login screen and cannot access the application without re-authentication

Title: Successful User Registration
Preconditions: None
Test Data: Valid email, password, and required user details
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the registration page
2. Fill in all required fields with valid data
3. Submit the registration form
Expected Result: User account is created in the database and confirmation message is displayed

Title: Search for a City and Display POI/Events
Preconditions: User must be logged in
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the city search form
2. Enter a valid city name
3. Submit the search
Expected Result: Application displays the city page with POI and events (RF-04)

Title: Search for POI Types
Preconditions: User must be logged in and on a city page
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the POI search section
2. Select a POI type from the dropdown list
Expected Result: Application displays a list of POI of the selected type (RF-05, RF-06)

Title: Create a New Event in a City
Preconditions: User must be logged in and on a city page (RF-03)
Test Data: Valid event details (name, date, description, etc.)
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the city page
2. Click the "Create Event" button
3. Fill in all required event details
4. Submit the form
Expected Result: Event is created and appears in the user’s "My Events" section (RF-07)

Title: Modify an Existing Event
Preconditions: User must be logged in and be the creator of the event
Test Data: Updated event details (e.g., new date, description)
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the "My Events" section
2. Select an event to modify
3. Edit event details
4. Submit changes
Expected Result: Event details are updated and changes are reflected in the system (RF-08)

Title: Delete an Existing Event
Preconditions: User must be logged in and be the creator of the event
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the "My Events" section
2. Select an event to delete
3. Confirm deletion
Expected Result: Event is removed from the system and no longer appears in "My Events" (RF-09)

Title: View Events for a Selected City
Preconditions: User has searched for a city and is on the city information screen with POI (Points of Interest) displayed.
Test Data: A city with at least one ongoing or future event (e.g., "Rome" with "Colosseum Tour" on 2024-06-15).
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the city information screen (e.g., Rome).
2. User selects the option to view events for the city.
3. System displays a list of events (ongoing or future) associated with the city.
Expected Result: The list of events is displayed correctly, showing event names, dates, and descriptions.

Title: User Logout Functionality
Preconditions: User is logged into the application.
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the logout option (e.g., profile menu or settings).
2. User confirms the logout action (if a confirmation dialog appears).
3. System logs the user out of the account.
Expected Result: User is successfully logged out, and the login screen appears. The user must re-authenticate to access the application again.

Title: Application Installation Space Requirement
Preconditions: Device has at least 50 MB of free storage space.
Test Steps:
1. User initiates the application download/installation process.
2. System checks for available storage space on the device.
Expected Result: Installation proceeds successfully without errors if space is sufficient (≥50 MB). If space is insufficient, an error message is displayed.

Title: Successful Application Installation
Preconditions: Device has sufficient storage space (≥50 MB).
Test Steps:
1. User completes the download process.
2. System installs the application on the device.
Expected Result: Application is installed correctly and is accessible from the device’s app launcher.

Title: Application Uninstallation and Data Removal
Preconditions: Application is installed on the device.
Test Steps:
1. User initiates the uninstallation process for the application.
2. System removes the application and all associated data from the device.
Expected Result: Application is uninstalled, and no residual data (e.g., caches, databases) remains on the device. Device storage space is freed up.

Title: Database/External Component Communication Stability
Preconditions: Application is connected to the database and external components (e.g., Google Maps).
Test Steps:
1. User performs an action requiring database or external component communication (e.g., viewing city events or maps).
2. System maintains stable communication without interruptions.
Expected Result: The action completes successfully without errors or disconnections. If an interruption occurs, the system recovers gracefully or notifies the user.

Title: Successful User Registration
Preconditions: User has installed the application on an Android device (version 7.0+)
Test Data: Valid email, password, and required registration details
Test Steps:
1. Open the application for the first time.
2. Select the registration option.
3. Fill in the registration form with valid data (email, password, etc.).
4. Press the registration button.
Expected Result: The system confirms account creation and redirects the user to the city search screen.

Title: Valid City Search in Veneto
Preconditions: User is logged into the application
Test Data: Name of a valid city in Veneto (e.g., "Venezia")
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the city search screen.
2. Type the name of a valid city in Veneto.
3. Select the city from the dropdown or autocomplete suggestions.
Expected Result: The system displays the city’s data (POI, events) successfully.

Title: Invalid City Search (Non-Veneto City)
Preconditions: User is logged into the application
Test Data: Name of a city outside Veneto (e.g., "Roma")
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the city search screen.
2. Type the name of a city not in Veneto.
3. Select the city from the dropdown or autocomplete suggestions.
Expected Result: The system displays an error message indicating the city is not in Veneto.

Title: Application Installation on Android 7.0+
Preconditions: None
Test Data: Android device with version 7.0 or higher
Test Steps:
1. Attempt to install the application on an Android device running version 7.0 or higher.
2. Complete the installation process.
Expected Result: The application installs successfully without errors and runs without issues.

Title: Application Functionality with Internet Connectivity
Preconditions: Application is installed and opened
Test Steps:
1. Open the application.
2. Attempt to perform any action requiring internet (e.g., login, city search).
Expected Result: The application functions correctly without errors, allowing access to all features.

Title: Application Behavior Without Internet Connectivity
Preconditions: Application is installed and opened
Test Steps:
1. Open the application.
2. Attempt to perform an action requiring internet (e.g., login, city search).
Expected Result: The application displays an error message indicating the need for an internet connection.

Title: Secure Data Transmission During Login
Preconditions: User is attempting to log in
Test Steps:
1. Open the application and navigate to the login screen.
2. Enter valid credentials.
3. Press the login button (triggering data transmission).
Expected Result: The data is transmitted securely using encrypted protocols (e.g., HTTPS, TLS).

Title: Secure Data Transmission During Registration
Preconditions: User is attempting to register
Test Steps:
1. Open the application and navigate to the registration screen.
2. Fill in valid registration details.
3. Press the registration button (triggering data transmission).
Expected Result: The data is transmitted securely using encrypted protocols (e.g., HTTPS, TLS).

Title: Application Installation Storage Requirement
Preconditions: None
Test Data: Android device with at least 200 MB free storage
Test Steps: 1. Attempt to install the application on a device with 200 MB or more free storage.
Expected Result: The application installs successfully without storage-related errors.

Title: Application Installation on Low Storage Device
Preconditions: None
Test Data: Android device with less than 200 MB free storage
Test Steps: 1. Attempt to install the application on a device with less than 200 MB free storage.
Expected Result: The installation fails with an error message indicating insufficient storage.

Title: Retrieve previously searched city information
Preconditions: User has searched for an existing city in Veneto
Test Steps:
1. User searches for a city in Veneto (e.g., "Venezia").
2. User navigates to the section where previously searched cities are displayed.
Expected Result: The application displays the previously searched city information.

Title: Search for POI types and display results
Preconditions: User is logged in and has selected the POI search functionality
Test Steps:
1. User selects a POI type (e.g., "Ristoranti").
2. User clicks the search button.
Expected Result: The application displays the list of POI of the selected type in Veneto.

Title: Error handling for no POI type selection
Preconditions: User is logged in and has selected the POI search functionality
Test Steps: 1. User clicks the search button without selecting any POI type.
Expected Result: The application displays an error message indicating no type was selected.

Title: Display previously searched POI list
Preconditions: User has completed the POI search form and clicked the search button
Test Steps: 1. User completes the POI search form and clicks the search button.
Expected Result: The application displays a list of the desired POI types.

Title: Create a new event for a city
Preconditions: User is logged in and has searched for a city
Test Data: Valid event details (e.g., start date: "2024-06-01", end date: "2024-06-02", type: "Concerto", description: "Concerto di musica classica")
Test Steps:
1. User clicks the "Crea evento" button.
2. User fills in the event form with start date, end date, type, and optional description.
3. User submits the form.
Expected Result: The application adds the new event associated with the searched city.

Title: Delete an event from a city
Preconditions: User is logged in with the account of the event creator
Test Steps:
1. User clicks the "Cancella evento" button for the event.
2. User confirms the deletion.
Expected Result: The application removes the event and updates the list of events for the city.

Title: View list of events in a city
Preconditions: User is on the city search page (Home)
Test Steps:
1. User searches for a city (e.g., "Verona").
2. User navigates to the events section for that city.
Expected Result: The application displays all registered events for the city.

Title: Logout from the application
Preconditions: User is logged in
Test Steps: 1. User clicks the logout button.
Expected Result: The application displays the login page and the user must log in again to access functionalities.
